_meta.description: Book-authenticity + idiom/pattern fidelity verdicts per named claim, per arm. Graded by the `oracle` agent against the git-ignored .oracle/ books (Fowler refactoring-2e, Kerievsky refactoring-to-patterns, GoF design-patterns). DST-04: main context never read book prose; all justifications are the oracle's own words. FP idioms are no-oracle (graded vs functional-depatterning-ts.md + the shipped functional-catalog).
_meta.oracle_caveat: Graded a deduplicated vocabulary list, not raw per-cell transcripts; whether each code_review smell label attached to the correct construct is corroborated by the claims-normalizer's read of the actual outputs + code, not re-verified by the oracle.

smells.why: Exact Fowler 2e ch.3 smell (Kerievsky uses the older Long Method).
Named smell in both Fowler ch.3 and Kerievsky ch.4.
Not a canonically named smell; target of a 1st-ed Fowler refactoring dropped from the owned 2e; sits under Duplicated Code.
Exact Fowler 2e ch.3 smell (Kerievsky equivalent: Switch Statements).
Named smell in Fowler ch.3 and Kerievsky ch.4.
Genuine Fowler 2e ch.3 smell (new in 2e; routed to Replace Loop with Pipeline).
Not a standalone named smell; folded under Primitive Obsession / Mysterious Name. Real concern, non-canonical name.
Not a named smell; concept lives inside Kerievsky Conditional Complexity, addressed by guard-clause/decompose refactorings.

refactorings.why: Fowler 2e catalog; correct primary cure for Long Function / Duplicated Code.
Genuine 1st-ed Fowler refactoring, absent from owned 2e; 2e successor is Replace Function with Command. Concept faithful, name superseded.
Kerievsky's refactoring is Compose Method; Composed Method is the Beck pattern it targets. Right concept, slightly off name.
Fowler 2e catalog; correct for Data Clumps / long parameter lists.
Fowler 2e catalog; correct cure for the Loops smell (the FP-axis target T3).
Genuine 1st-ed Fowler refactoring; not in owned 2e (subsumed by Slide Statements + consolidation). Legacy name.
Not a named book refactoring; maps to the Extract Class family (TS-flavored variant). Faithful concept, non-canonical name.
Fowler 2e catalog (also Kerievsky's cure for Oddball Solution).

refactorings.item: Replace Nested Conditional with Guard Clauses

refactorings.why: Neither exact name in owned 2e (1e name: Replace Magic Number with Symbolic Constant). Concept faithful, name non-canonical.
Fowler 2e catalog (routed before Replace Conditional with Polymorphism for a type code).

patterns.why: Genuine Fowler 2e refactoring; Fowler gates it (only when the same switch repeats), so declining for simple conditionals and splitting on growth is book-consistent.
Exact Kerievsky ch.7 refactoring (Strategy); gating/declining on net-cost matches his over-engineering caution.
Exact Kerievsky ch.7 refactoring (State); declining unless genuinely complex is consistent.
Genuine GoF pattern; declining for ordinary conditional logic matches GoF applicability.

patterns.item: Replace Type Code with Subclasses (gated behind growth)
patterns.why: Genuine Fowler refactoring; gating behind growth is faithful to YAGNI-aware framing.
Authentic in Kerievsky (Introduce Null Object) and Fowler 2e (Introduce Special Case); declining when null-handling not duplicated is book-consistent.
Genuine Kerievsky/Beck pattern (target of Move Accumulation to Collecting Parameter).
Pure functional idiom, not one of the owned OO books; correctly not treated as a book pattern.
Functional idiom (functional-catalog / functional-depatterning-ts.md), not an owned-book pattern.
FP idiom; the OO-book-graded counterpart is the refactoring Replace Loop with Pipeline (graded pass above).

patterns.item: polymorphism / per-item strategy map (candidate, no APPLY/DECLINE)
patterns.why: The pattern named is a real concept, but floated without a net-cost weighing -- the unguarded framing Kerievsky's balance warns against; the coverage is authentic, the proportionality guidance is absent.

invoke_skill.note: Fowler mechanical refactorings + Kerievsky routing authentic; declining Replace Conditional with Polymorphism here is book-consistent.

code_review.note: Named smells drawn from Fowler ch.3 (authentic); no refactoring/pattern vocabulary -- a coverage gap, not a fidelity failure.

invoke_skill.note: Authentic; declining Strategy/polymorphism on net-cost mirrors Kerievsky's caution + GoF applicability; the Map/lookup form is a no-oracle FP idiom, correctly not booked as a pattern.

code_review.note: Fowler ch.3 smell names authentic; no canonical refactoring/pattern names (coverage, not fidelity).

invoke_skill.note: Growth scenario book-consistent: gating Replace Type Code with Subclasses behind actual growth + split verdict on Replace Conditional with Polymorphism matches Fowler's Repeated-Switches rule + Kerievsky's balance.

code_review.note: Fowler ch.3 smell naming authentic; absence of refactoring/pattern vocabulary is expected coverage, not a fidelity defect; the one named pattern (polymorphism/strategy map) is floated unguarded (partial on proportionality).

cross_cutting: invoke_skill routing is faithful to the books (mechanical/local -> Fowler catalog; repeated/complex-structure -> Kerievsky; GoF/Kerievsky patterns only when warranted, otherwise declined on net-cost). The only fidelity blemishes are legacy 1st-ed names (Replace Method with Method Object, Consolidate Duplicate Conditional Fragments) and TS-flavored labels (Extract Type, Extract Constant) -- concepts faithful, names off-catalog; all graded partial, none fail. code_review names authentic Fowler ch.3 smells and no canonical refactoring/pattern vocabulary. No fail verdicts on either arm.
